title: Fix $typename return on `array.min` etc
body:
#### Example

```sv
module m;
  int unsigned x[3] = '{0, 1, 2};
  initial begin
    $display(x.min);
    $finish;
  end
endmodule
```

Run:

```sh
verilator -MAKEFLAGS '-s' --quiet --binary m.sv && obj_dir/Vm +verilator+quiet
```

Result:

```
2302531392            (comment: changes after every execution)
- m.sv:5: Verilog $finish
```

Expected:

```
'{'h0}        (comment: array locator methods return a queue)
- m.sv:5: Verilog $finish
```

Version and OS

```
Verilator 5.024 2024-04-05
Archlinux
```
---

#### Notes

I could only find tests for fixed-sized size unpacked arrays but:
1. for associative arrays

     https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/1315aa31ed1b980b05778f5a0a1f3ff9718be680/test_regress/t/t_assoc_method.v#L90

2. for queues
   
      https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/1315aa31ed1b980b05778f5a0a1f3ff9718be680/test_regress/t/t_queue_method.v#L161

So using a queue instead works as expected:

```sv
module m;
  int unsigned x[$] = '{0, 1, 2};
  initial begin
    $display(x.min);
    $finish;
  end
endmodule
```
Result:

```
'{'h0}
- m.sv:5: Verilog $finish
```

`min` and `max` are defined in *LRM 2023 7.12.1 Array locator methods*. AFAI understand these must be defined for all arrays.

---

Currently I don't have the capacity for a PR other than preparing a test case.
